methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
CubeSpace is a Growth-stage company operating in Space & Aerospace. The valuation exercise draws from a peer universe of 13 comparables: 5 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private firms including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Two standard triangulation methods are not applicable here. Peer-Median analysis fails due to insufficient narrow-filter matches: while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering for identical stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yields fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical validity. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because CubeSpace's revenue is not on file—a common constraint in private company analysis. The public Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 45.3x P/S median across the 6 comparable tickers, but without the target's revenue figure, this anchor cannot be operationalized.

Given the lack of quantitative datapoints from either method, a definitive valuation range cannot be derived from this analysis. The primary uncertainty is disclosure: CubeSpace's revenue and profitability metrics are required to unlock public multiple anchoring or more refined peer-median work. Until such operational data surfaces or a tighter cluster of same-stage private comps is identified with disclosed valuations, further valuation work remains limited to qualitative stage and sector positioning rather than quantitative synthesis.
